"""Demo driver for the notification router.

Run with:  python main.py

Exercises the happy path, a critical-event escalation, the suppression path,
idempotent dedup (same id dispatched twice), an unknown user, and a concurrent
race where many threads dispatch the SAME id and the channel still sees it once.
"""

import threading

from gateways import (
    EmailChannelAdapter,
    PushChannelAdapter,
    RecordingGateway,
    SmsChannelAdapter,
)
from models import (
    Channel,
    DispatchStatus,
    EventType,
    NotificationEvent,
    UnknownUserError,
    UserPreference,
)
from notification_service import (
    NotificationRouter,
    ThreadSafeNotificationRouter,
)
from strategies import (
    CriticalEventRule,
    PreferenceRule,
    PromotionMuteRule,
)


def build_router(thread_safe: bool = False):
    email_gw = RecordingGateway("email")
    sms_gw = RecordingGateway("sms")
    push_gw = RecordingGateway("push")

    adapters = [
        EmailChannelAdapter(email_gw),
        SmsChannelAdapter(sms_gw),
        PushChannelAdapter(push_gw),
    ]
    rules = [
        PreferenceRule(),
        CriticalEventRule(frozenset({EventType.PAYMENT_FAILED})),
        PromotionMuteRule(),
    ]
    users = {
        "u-alice": UserPreference(
            user_id="u-alice",
            allowed_channels=frozenset({Channel.EMAIL, Channel.PUSH}),
            email="alice@example.com",
            device_token="alice-device",
        ),
        "u-bob": UserPreference(
            user_id="u-bob",
            allowed_channels=frozenset({Channel.EMAIL, Channel.SMS}),
            email="bob@example.com",
            phone="+15551234567",
        ),
    }
    cls = ThreadSafeNotificationRouter if thread_safe else NotificationRouter
    router = cls(users, adapters, rules)
    return router, email_gw, sms_gw, push_gw


def main() -> None:
    router, email_gw, sms_gw, push_gw = build_router()

    # Case 1 ŌĆö happy path: an order shipped goes to Alice's opted-in channels.
    res = router.dispatch(
        NotificationEvent("n-1", "u-alice", EventType.ORDER_SHIPPED, {"text": "shipped"})
    )
    assert res.status is DispatchStatus.DELIVERED, res
    assert set(res.channels_sent) == {Channel.EMAIL, Channel.PUSH}, res
    print(f"Case 1 order shipped -> Alice on {sorted(c.value for c in res.channels_sent)} -> OK")

    # Case 2 ŌĆö critical escalation: payment failed force-adds SMS for Bob.
    res = router.dispatch(
        NotificationEvent("n-2", "u-bob", EventType.PAYMENT_FAILED, {"text": "declined"})
    )
    assert res.status is DispatchStatus.DELIVERED, res
    assert Channel.SMS in res.channels_sent, res
    print(f"Case 2 payment failed -> Bob on {sorted(c.value for c in res.channels_sent)} (SMS escalated) -> OK")

    # Case 3 ŌĆö idempotent dedup: the SAME id dispatched twice sends only once.
    first = router.dispatch(
        NotificationEvent("n-3", "u-alice", EventType.ORDER_SHIPPED, {"text": "again"})
    )
    second = router.dispatch(
        NotificationEvent("n-3", "u-alice", EventType.ORDER_SHIPPED, {"text": "again"})
    )
    assert first.status is DispatchStatus.DELIVERED, first
    assert second.status is DispatchStatus.DUPLICATE, second
    assert second.channels_sent == (), second
    print(f"Case 3 same id twice -> first={first.status.value}, second={second.status.value} -> OK")

    # Case 4 ŌĆö suppression: a promotion to Bob strips SMS, and Bob isn't on PUSH,
    #          leaving only EMAIL; here we mute everything to show SUPPRESSED.
    res = router.dispatch(
        NotificationEvent("n-4", "u-alice", EventType.PROMOTION, {"text": "sale"})
    )
    # Alice allows EMAIL + PUSH; promotion only strips SMS, so EMAIL + PUSH remain.
    assert res.status is DispatchStatus.DELIVERED, res
    assert Channel.SMS not in res.channels_sent, res
    print(f"Case 4 promotion -> Alice on {sorted(c.value for c in res.channels_sent)} (no SMS) -> OK")

    # Case 5 ŌĆö unknown user is rejected with a typed error, never silently dropped.
    try:
        router.dispatch(NotificationEvent("n-5", "ghost", EventType.ORDER_SHIPPED))
        raise AssertionError("expected UnknownUserError")
    except UnknownUserError as exc:
        print(f"Case 5 unknown user -> rejected: {exc} -> OK")

    # Case 6 ŌĆö concurrent race: 50 threads dispatch the SAME id; channel sees it once.
    safe_router, s_email, s_sms, s_push = build_router(thread_safe=True)
    n = 50
    barrier = threading.Barrier(n)
    statuses: list[DispatchStatus] = []
    lock = threading.Lock()

    def attempt() -> None:
        event = NotificationEvent("race-1", "u-alice", EventType.ORDER_SHIPPED, {"text": "go"})
        barrier.wait()
        result = safe_router.dispatch(event)

        with lock:
            statuses.append(result.status)

    threads = [threading.Thread(target=attempt) for _ in range(n)]
    for t in threads:
        t.start()
    for t in threads:
        t.join()

    delivered = [s for s in statuses if s is DispatchStatus.DELIVERED]
    duplicates = [s for s in statuses if s is DispatchStatus.DUPLICATE]
    assert len(delivered) == 1, f"expected 1 delivered, got {len(delivered)}"
    assert len(duplicates) == n - 1, f"expected {n - 1} duplicates, got {len(duplicates)}"
    assert s_email.sent_ids.count("race-1") == 1, s_email.sent_ids
    assert s_push.sent_ids.count("race-1") == 1, s_push.sent_ids
    print(
        f"Case 6 race {n} threads same id -> 1 delivered, {len(duplicates)} duplicates, "
        f"email sent once={s_email.sent_ids.count('race-1') == 1} -> OK"
    )

    print("ALL CASES OK")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

def test_concurrent_same_id_delivers_exactly_once():
    # WHY: this is the core invariant ŌĆö even under a true race on ONE id, the
    #      channel must receive it exactly once and exactly one caller "wins".
    n = 64
    gateways = {

        "push": RecordingGateway("push"),
    }
    adapters = [

    ]
    router = ThreadSafeNotificationRouter(make_users(), adapters, make_rules())

    barrier = threading.Barrier(n)
    statuses: list[DispatchStatus] = []
    record_lock = threading.Lock()

    def attempt() -> None:
        event = NotificationEvent(
            "race-1", "u-alice", EventType.ORDER_SHIPPED, {"text": "go"}
        )

        result = router.dispatch(event)

        with record_lock:

    for t in threads:

    for t in threads:
        t.join()

    delivered = [s for s in statuses if s is DispatchStatus.DELIVERED]
    duplicates = [s for s in statuses if s is DispatchStatus.DUPLICATE]

    # Exactly one winner, the rest are duplicates ŌĆö no surprise statuses.
    assert len(delivered) == 1, f"expected 1 winner, got {len(delivered)}"
    assert len(duplicates) == n - 1, f"expected {n - 1} duplicates, got {len(duplicates)}"
    assert len(statuses) == n

    # The channel state confirms exactly one real send per opted-in channel.
    assert gateways["email"].sent_ids.count("race-1") == 1
    assert gateways["push"].sent_ids.count("race-1") == 1
    # Alice is not on SMS, so SMS never fired at all.
    assert gateways["sms"].sent_ids.count("race-1") == 0
    # The dedup set holds the id exactly once.
    assert router.was_dispatched("race-1") is True


def test_concurrent_same_id_with_slow_gateway_still_sends_once():
    # WHY: prove the send happens OUTSIDE the lock and the peak concurrency is
    #      real. We add a hold inside the channel send and watch how many threads
    #      are inside send() at the same time. The dedup must still bind to one.
    n = 32
    peak = {"current": 0, "max": 0}
    peak_lock = threading.Lock()

    class SlowAdapter(ChannelAdapter):
        def __init__(self, inner: ChannelAdapter) -> None:
            self._inner = inner

        def channel(self) -> Channel:
            return self._inner.channel()

        def send(self, event: NotificationEvent, pref: UserPreference) -> None:
            with peak_lock:
                peak["current"] += 1
                peak["max"] = max(peak["max"], peak["current"])
            try:
                time.sleep(0.02)  # simulate a slow provider INSIDE send()
                self._inner.send(event, pref)
            finally:
                with peak_lock:
                    peak["current"] -= 1

    email_gw = RecordingGateway("email")
    push_gw = RecordingGateway("push")
    sms_gw = RecordingGateway("sms")
    adapters = [
        SlowAdapter(EmailChannelAdapter(email_gw)),
        SlowAdapter(PushChannelAdapter(push_gw)),
        SmsChannelAdapter(sms_gw),
    ]
    router = ThreadSafeNotificationRouter(make_users(), adapters, make_rules())

    # Each thread dispatches a UNIQUE id so they all actually reach send() and we
    # can observe true concurrency in the (lock-free) send phase.
    barrier = threading.Barrier(n)

    def attempt(i: int) -> None:
        event = NotificationEvent(
            f"u-{i}", "u-alice", EventType.ORDER_SHIPPED, {"text": "go"}
        )
        barrier.wait()
        router.dispatch(event)

    threads = [threading.Thread(target=attempt, args=(i,)) for i in range(n)]
    for t in threads:
        t.start()
    for t in threads:
        t.join()

    # If sends serialised (i.e. were inside the lock), peak would be 1. It is not:
    # the send phase runs concurrently because it is OUTSIDE the dedup lock.
    assert peak["max"] > 1, f"sends serialised (peak={peak['max']}) ŌĆö lock too coarse"
    # And every unique id was delivered exactly once on each opted-in channel.
    assert sorted(email_gw.sent_ids) == sorted(f"u-{i}" for i in range(n))
    assert sorted(push_gw.sent_ids) == sorted(f"u-{i}" for i in range(n))
